description_long: This is a sleek, futuristic airplane with a long, pointed nose and a streamlined fuselage, reminiscent of the Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird. The aircraft features two large, cylindrical jet engines mounted on either side of the fuselage, each with visible exhaust nozzles at the rear. Its wings are sharply swept back and blend smoothly into the body, giving it a delta-like appearance with additional stabilizing surfaces near the tail. The tail section includes twin vertical stabilizers set wide apart, connected by a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with a smooth canopy. The overall surface is smooth and metallic, with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle blue highlights near the cockpit.
description: A sleek, gray, jet-powered airplane with swept wings, twin engine nacelles, and a pointed nose, featuring a forward-mounted enclosed cockpit and twin vertical stabilizers.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit, and the wide spacing of the twin engine nacelles.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long, slender fuselage, sharply swept wings, and the alignment of the engines with the body.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the twin engine exhausts, wide-set vertical stabilizers, and the horizontal stabilizer connecting them.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the streamlined profile and engine placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept wings with blended wing-body design and no visible winglets; slight anhedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted in nacelles on either side of the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a connecting horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear not visibly extended in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a streamlined canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
